title: Grandpa Frank Reuben Sandwiches
ingredients: Good-quality pastrami, sliced very thin (Boar's Head brand recommended)
1 tbsp horseradish (fresh if possible, not creamed)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Make Russian Dressing: Mix mayonnaise, ketchup, hot sauce, horseradish, Worcestershire sauce, smoked paprika, chives, and minced garlic in a bowl. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Refrigerate until ready to use.
Butter the outsides of two slices of marble rye bread.
Generously coat the inside of each bread slice with Russian dressing.
Place sliced Swiss cheese next to the dressing on the inside of both bread slices (cheese serves as glue to hold sandwich together).
Top one bread slice with drained sauerkraut.
Stack a generous amount of pastrami on top of the sauerkraut.
Top with the other slice of marble rye bread, dressing-side and cheese-side down.
Grill the sandwich on both sides until toasted.
Serve with dill pickles and potato chips.
notes: This is an interpretation of a Reuben that Grandpa Frank would have loved
Best eaten while playing Monopoly and watching football – Grandpa Frank's favorite games
Use fresh horseradish if possible, not creamed horseradish
Growing fresh chives in your backyard is recommended for the Russian dressing
Make Russian dressing ahead and refrigerate until ready to use
